project.name: journey-to-recovery
project.description: A full-stack stroke rehabilitation platform with SMART goals, wellness tracking, and an AI-powered chatbot.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Express API server, configuring CORS, JSON and cookie middleware before mounting the application's route modules under /api.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Express middleware composition and environment-specific CORS configuration.

nodes.name: NavBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the persistent client navigation bar with route-aware links and a clinician-only alerts indicator. It refreshes the alert count when the clinician or route changes.

nodes.name: NavBar
nodes.summary: Builds the route navigation controls and conditionally retrieves and displays the clinician alert count.

nodes.name: alert.test.ts
nodes.summary: Exercises clinician alert endpoints, covering authorization, alert retrieval, status transitions, validation, and database failure responses.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Contributor guidance for the Journey to Recovery monorepo, covering commands, architecture, testing conventions, environment configuration, and deployment operations.

nodes.name: HANDOFF.md
nodes.summary: Project handoff for the completed risk-escalation pipeline, documenting alert flow, review fixes, operational gotchas, and outstanding manual verification work.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Defines the Bun-based Express server package, its API and database dependencies, and commands for development, tests, evaluation workflows, and daily email digests.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses native ES modules and Bun scripts to run the TypeScript server without a separate compilation command.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Combines conditional class names and merges Tailwind CSS utility conflicts.

nodes.name: Signup.tsx
nodes.summary: React registration page that validates account details, invokes the authentication signup action, and displays submission errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React Hook Form with a Zod resolver for typed client-side validation.

nodes.name: Signup
nodes.summary: Renders the account creation form and submits validated credentials through the authentication context.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Defines reusable Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types for registration, login, profile, and strengths forms.
nodes.languageNotes: Zod schemas are refined and inferred to keep form validation and TypeScript types aligned.

nodes.name: routes.tsx
nodes.summary: Builds the client-side route tree, placing public account pages and authenticated wellbeing workflows under the shared layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React Router's data-router API to compose nested layout, private-route, and clinician-route branches.

nodes.name: axiosConfig.ts
nodes.summary: Configures the shared Axios API client and retries unauthorized requests after refreshing the session token.

nodes.name: connection.ts
nodes.summary: Creates the MySQL connection pool and exports the Drizzle database client, including a UTC session setting that preserves timestamp interpretation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses mysql2/promise with Drizzle's MySQL adapter and sets the connection session timezone explicitly.

nodes.name: alertRoutes.ts
nodes.summary: Defines clinician-protected alert endpoints for listing open and historical alerts, retrieving counts and details, and acknowledging or resolving alerts.

nodes.name: authRoutes.ts
nodes.summary: Implements registration, clinician registration, login, refresh-token rotation, and logout endpoints using JWTs, secure cookies, password hashing, and token revocation.

nodes.name: issueTokens
nodes.summary: Signs access and refresh JWTs, persists the refresh token, and writes both tokens to HTTP cookies.

nodes.name: chatRoutes.ts
nodes.summary: Provides authenticated conversation endpoints that moderate and sanitize prompts, call an LLM with structured SMART-goal output, persist chat state, calculate risk, and create alerts when needed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a typed transaction alias to coordinate conversational writes and Zod validation to retry malformed LLM JSON responses.

nodes.name: createAlert
nodes.summary: Creates a risk-related alert inside a database transaction and forces HIGH severity for direct safety-flag messages.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Exports Zod schemas and inferred TypeScript types for authentication, profiles, check-ins, goals, wellness assessments, chat prompts, and clinician alert updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Zod schemas are paired with inferred TypeScript input types to keep request validation and handler typing aligned.

nodes.name: ChatSimulator.ts
nodes.summary: Supplies a deterministic direct-LLM simulator for evaluation scenarios, supporting Gemini and OpenAI providers, structured SMART-goal parsing, conversation history, and usage capture.

nodes.name: ChatSimulator
nodes.summary: Runs multi-turn, reproducible LLM conversations and reports parsed responses, token usage, completion state, and evaluation errors.

nodes.name: prompt.config.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the structured SMART-goal response contract, strict provider-neutral JSON schema, and safety-focused system prompt for the Camay rehabilitation assistant.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains both a Zod runtime schema and a hand-written strict JSON Schema so OpenAI and Gemini can enforce the same response contract.

nodes.name: _testUtils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides shared Bun test scaffolding: mocked database and AI clients, a fully wired Express app, authentication cookies, and mock-reset helpers for server route tests.

nodes.name: chat.test.ts
nodes.summary: Exercises chat and conversation endpoints across success, validation, moderation, transaction-failure, and risk-alert scenarios using the shared mocked server environment.

nodes.name: startServer
nodes.summary: Starts the shared Express test app on an ephemeral port and returns its server and base URL.

nodes.name: stopServer
nodes.summary: Closes a test HTTP server after a route test suite completes.

nodes.name: signTestAccessToken
nodes.summary: Creates a short-lived JWT access token using the test secret.

nodes.name: signTestRefreshToken
nodes.summary: Creates a test refresh token using the dedicated refresh-token secret.

nodes.name: authCookie
nodes.summary: Builds the access-token cookie header required by authenticated route tests.

nodes.name: decodeAccessTokenCookie
nodes.summary: Extracts and decodes the access token from a Set-Cookie response header for assertions.

nodes.name: mockAuthOk
nodes.summary: Queues an empty blacklist lookup so middleware treats a test request as authenticated.

nodes.name: resetMocks
nodes.summary: Clears all shared database, AI, transaction, and connection mocks between tests.

nodes.name: baseSmartGoalResponse
nodes.summary: Builds a representative SMART-goal AI response that tests can selectively override.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Documents the server's local and production environment variables for MySQL, JWT signing, AI providers, runtime settings, clinician registration, and Resend email delivery.

layers.name: Client Experience
layers.description: The React application: recovery workflows, authentication screens, routing, shared UI primitives, styling, and client-side integrations.

layers.name: Server Application
layers.description: The Express API implementation: request handling, authentication, recovery-domain operations, validation, integrations, and operational jobs.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Database connectivity, schema definitions, and SQL migrations that persist recovery-platform data.

layers.name: Quality & Evaluation
layers.description: Server route tests, chatbot evaluation runners, fixtures, reports, and scenario datasets.

layers.name: Configuration & Tooling
layers.description: Repository and package manifests, build settings, environment templates, and local-development tooling.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Repository and package documentation covering architecture, contribution context, handoff, and server/client usage.

layers.name: CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows that gate evaluations and promote approved baselines.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Read the repository overview and workspace manifest first. They explain the stroke-rehabilitation platform and establish the client, API, recovery workflows, and quality checks explored in this tour.

tour.title: Client Startup and Routing
tour.description: The client entry point mounts the application, and the routing tree makes public, protected, recovery, and clinician screens reachable. Together they provide the browser-facing map before examining individual workflows.
tour.languageLesson: React applications mount one root component and use declarative routes to select screen trees from the browser URL.

tour.title: Authentication and Recovery Workflows
tour.description: Authentication context supplies session-aware state to protected routes, while Axios configuration is the shared HTTP boundary. The SMART-goal and tracking screens show how a participant moves through a focused recovery workflow.

tour.title: Conversational Support
tour.description: The chatbot coordinates conversation state, message submission, goal context, and assistant responses. Its message and sidebar components form a stateful client feature that connects directly to the server's chat and safety path.

tour.title: Express API and Risk Escalation
tour.description: The server bootstraps middleware and mounts the API routes, with shared schemas validating requests at the boundary. Chat handling, risk calculation, and alert routes then turn conversational signals into clinician review workflows.
tour.languageLesson: Express middleware forms a request pipeline, allowing requests to be enriched, validated, or rejected before route handlers run.

tour.title: Persistent Recovery Data
tour.description: The database connection and migrations reveal the durable model behind accounts, goals, check-ins, conversations, and alerts. Read the goal and alert tables with the migration to connect API operations to the data they preserve.
tour.languageLesson: SQL migrations evolve a database in ordered, reviewable steps; tables and constraints preserve the relationships application routes rely on.

tour.title: Tests, Evaluation, and Release Gates
tour.description: Shared route-test utilities exercise server behavior through HTTP, while the evaluation runner checks chatbot scenarios against an approved baseline. The environment template and CI workflows document runtime needs and keep those safeguards repeatable for every change.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows separate triggers, jobs, and sequential steps, making evaluation gates repeatable in CI.
